start.description:
This tool helps you determine if you are up-to-date with your routine vaccinations in the UK.

⚠️**Vaccinations are important**⚠️  [Here is what you should know](https://www.immunology.org/public-information/vaccine-resources/childhood-vaccines/videos-about-vaccines/do-you-know-how)

*Please note: This is a demo tool and not an official NHS service.*

adult_path.text: Is this vaccination record up to date?
adult_path.description:
Please review your vaccination record.

Based on your knowledge, does this information look complete and up to date?

routine_check.text: Do you have any additional vaccination information not in our records?
routine_check.description: This may include vaccines received in childhood or in other countries that are not recorded here.

result_young_up_to_date.text: You appear to be up to date!
result_young_up_to_date.description: Based on your age, you have completed the standard UK childhood schedule. Keep an eye out for any future school-based invitations.

result_adult_protected.description: You have received your routine adult vaccinations. If you are planning to travel abroad, you may need additional travel-specific vaccines.

result_senior_fully_protected.text: Your vaccination records are up to date!!!
result_senior_fully_protected.description:
Based on your records, you may be due the following vaccines in line with the NHS vaccination programme.

You can learn more about recommended vaccines here: [NHS vaccinations and when to have them](https://www.nhs.uk/vaccinations/nhs-vaccinations-and-when-to-have-them/)

result_check_gp.description:
- **Check with a parent or guardian**
They may have a thing called a **Red Book**, a physical paper book used in the UK to track childhood health and vaccinations.

- **NHS App**
You may be able to view your vaccination record through the NHS App.  
[Find out more about the NHS App](https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-app/)

- **Contact your GP practice**
Your GP can access your NHS record and may be able to update it if you provide evidence of previous vaccinations.

- **Child Health Information Services (CHIS)** 
If you are in England, your childhood vaccination records may be held by your local CHIS.  
[Learn more about CHIS](https://chis.scwcsu.nhs.uk/about/contact-us)

- **Private healthcare providers**
If you received vaccinations privately, the provider may hold a record or be able to provide documentation.

- **Occupational health services**
If you were vaccinated through work (e.g. healthcare roles), your employer or occupational health team may have records.

- **Local vaccination services**
Some areas (e.g. Cardiff) offer local services that may hold or provide vaccination records.

nhsid.text: Do you have an NHS ID number?
nhsid.description:
This is different from a NI number, and helps GP's get access to your records.
We need this information to check that we are providing you with the correct details:

check_nhs_id.text: Enter your NHS number to check your record
check_nhs_id.description:
If you have an NHS number, you can use it to check your vaccination record.

For this demonstration, you can use the example NHS number below:
**9000000009**

If you do not know your NHS number, you can find it here:
[Find your NHS number](https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-services/find-nhs-number/)

newpatient.text: If you are new to the NHS
newpatient.description:
If you are new to the UK or have not used the NHS before, you may not have an NHS number.

To access NHS services and build your health record, you will need to register with a GP. Once registered, you will be given an NHS number.

If you have received vaccinations outside the UK, these may not appear in your NHS record and you may need to share this information with your GP.

**Register with a GP here:**
[Find a GP](https://www.nhs.uk/service-search/find-a-gp/)

findyournhsnumber.description:
If you have used the NHS before, you should already have an NHS number.

You can retrieve it online using the link below. This will allow you to access your health record and check your vaccination status.

[Find your NHS number](https://www.nhs.uk/nhs-services/find-nhs-number/)

If you are unable to find your NHS number, you may wish to contact your GP practice for further assistance.

options.text: Confirm whether your record is complete

compareyourrecords.text: Do your vaccination records match to the recommended program
